5. Why gno.land?:

We're linkednode, independent validator for blockchain projects who participate and contribute many network. We're experienced to deploy a services and maintain the server.
We're committed to maintaining a fair and transparent approach to commission rates, with a focus on ensuring the long-term success of the network and the best interests of our delegators.
We are interested in participating in the incentivized testnet for several reasons. Firstly, we see it as an opportunity to contribute to the development and improvement of the network by actively testing its functionality and identifying any potential bugs or vulnerabilities. By participating in the testnet, we hope to provide valuable feedback to the developers, ultimately leading to a more robust and reliable platform.

6. Contributions:

We are strives to providing documents guide, monitoring, public RPC services in the future to make things easier for other operators. We're actively support testnet networks for testing.

Why I want to validate on gno.land:
I am interested in supporting the growth of the gno.land ecosystem and learning more about the Gno technology. I enjoy participating in new blockchain networks, helping improve decentralization, and maintaining reliable infrastructure for the community. I believe gno.land has a unique vision, and I would be happy to contribute to its long-term development.

Contributions:
I maintain validator infrastructure with continuous monitoring, regular upgrades, backups, and security best practices. I also provide public infrastructure where applicable, create installation guides and documentation, share updates with the community, and assist other node operators when possible. I look forward to contributing through testing, feedback, infrastructure support, and active participation as the gno.land ecosystem continues to grow.

**Why We Are Interested in Validating on gno.land:**

Node Chicharito is focused on reliable validator operations and practical ecosystem support. We have supported gno.land with infrastructure, snapshots, public endpoints, and tools for node operators. The community-driven, builder-focused nature of gno.land aligns well with how we contribute across networks, and we'd be glad to continue supporting the ecosystem as a validator while expanding our tooling.

**Contributions & Plans:**

- Public archival endpoints

- Snapshots for fast node sync

- Installation guides and a cheat sheet

- Public peers and seed nodes

HaruNode | High-Availability Validator Infrastructure

### 🚀 Core Philosophy & Vision
HaruNode started its journey nearly two years ago with a deep passion for open-source development and blockchain infrastructure. Driven by a mindset of continuous learning and rigorous self-improvement, we work tirelessly every day to elevate our technical capabilities. Our ultimate goal is to evolve into one of the most reliable, efficient, and deeply committed ecosystem partners in the Web3 space.

### 🎯 Why gno.land?
We believe gno.land represents the next evolution of blockchain development, bringing Go directly to smart contracts via GnoVM. We aim to contribute through reliable infrastructure, developer support, and community education.

### 🛠️ Contributions
• Infrastructure: High-performance bare metal servers with near-zero downtime.
• Services: Public RPC, API, gRPC endpoints, and fast state-sync services.
• Community: Detailed Turkish documentation and guides for new developers.
• Governance: Active participation in all network proposals and ecosystem growth.

### 🔗 Digital Presence
• GitHub: https://github.com/logic-apex
• X (Twitter): https://x.com/Logic__Apex
• Telegram: https://t.me/harundrk

**Validator Name:** TRACE™

**Networks We Are Currently Validating:**
We operate our own proprietary bare-metal servers with full hardware redundancy. This ensures maximum uptime and prevents the centralization risks associated with cloud providers \(AWS/GCP\).
**Total AuM:**
Active validator in AtomOne, Juno, and CrossFi with a combined Total Value Locked (TVL) of over $350,000.
**Links to Our Digital Presence:**
* Website: trace-tm.tech  
* Public Monitoring: monitoring.trace-tm.tech  
* Custom Explorer: explorer.trace-tm.tech
**Why We Are Interested in Validating on gno.land:**
Why Gno.land\? Embracing the New Language Standard \"We are deeply committed to the evolution of blockchain-specific languages. While Go is the backbone of the Cosmos ecosystem, its non-deterministic elements have historically required complex workarounds. Gno.land’s deterministic interpreter represents a paradigm shift. By validating on Gno.land, we are not just supporting a network\; we are supporting the establishment of a new gold standard for smart contract development. We believe that Gno’s focus on readability, simplicity, and strict state management is the key to creating more secure and auditable decentralized applications. Our goal is to provide the high-performance infrastructure necessary to make this new standard a reality.
**Contact Details:**
* Email: trace.valoper@gmail.com
* Twitter: @vovscorp
* Discord: @vovscorp
* GitHub: vovscorp13

serverType
on-prem/dedicated VPS

Huginn Tech is an infrastructure-first validator and tooling operator. Validator: Huginn Networks: We have operated validators and relayers for about three years across several L1s, including Cosmos Hub, Monad, Starknet, Polkadot, Axelar, Initia, Canton and Espresso. Our focus is uptime, secure key management and disciplined day-to-day operations. Production validators carry foundation and community delegations; newer networks are at testnet stage. Total AuM across networks is around $25M. We run our own infrastructure stack behind this: snapshots, public RPC and WS endpoints, addrbooks, live-peer lists, monitoring dashboards, custom staking and data APIs, and real-time alerting. Presence: huginn.tech, github.com/Huginntech, blog.huginn.tech, huginn.tv, x.com/HuginnStake, GnoLand Turkey t.me/GnoLandTR. Discord: gokay5690. Contact: Telegram [@sourled](https://topaz.testnets.gno.land/u/sourled), gokay@huginn.tech. Why gno.land: We have spent years running Tendermint-based infrastructure, so the TM2 base feels familiar, but the contribution-first model and the Gno and GnoVM design are what interest us. Keeping readable source on-chain is a different way of running a network, and we want to support that early. As operators we care about reliability over yield, and Proof-of-Contribution lines up with how we already work. Contributions: We build and maintain open-source operator tooling and public services, including monitoring, alerting, snapshots, public RPC and WS endpoints, dashboards and custom APIs for the networks we run. We produce technical guides and content in Turkish and English, run an active Turkish validator and operator community, and organize local meetups and ecosystem events. For gno.land we plan to run public endpoints, build node and validator monitoring tooling, write Turkish onboarding guides, and produce developer content on GnoVM and realms.
